data.title: Coinbase predicts 300% surge in stablecoin market cap to $1.2 trillion by 2028, based on US Treasuries model.
data.description: A US-based cryptocurrency exchange, Coinbase, is projecting a substantial increase in the stablecoin market cap, potentially reaching $1.2 trillion by 2028, marking a 300% surge from the current level. This forecast is informed by a model considering variables like the volume of US Treasuries issued, given that stablecoins are commonly backed by US dollars or Treasuries. According to Coinbase's analysis, this growth doesn't require significant or sustained rate disruptions but relies on gradual, policy-driven adoption accumulating over time. The exchange emphasizes that the market for dollar-pegged crypto assets is at a critical stage, with the potential for accelerated adoption.

data.title: El Salvador sees hundreds graduate with Bitcoin diplomas, 350 female students complete course. Initiative led by National Bitcoin Office.
data.description: In El Salvador, 350 female students graduated from the Bitcoin Diploma course at Instituto Nacional General Francisco Morazán. The initiative is part of the country's efforts to educate citizens on Bitcoin. The initiative is run by the National Bitcoin Office and has been extended to other institutions in the country. The Bitcoin Diploma was created by Mi Primer Bitcoin and provides free education materials covering the history of money, fiat currencies, and Bitcoin. The country's education minister, Karla Trigueros, attended the graduation ceremony. This reflects El Salvador's broader push to ensure its citizens understand the leading cryptocurrency. Initiatives like this aim to further integrate Bitcoin into the country's economy and daily life.

data.title: Global liquid biopsy market valued at USD 3.65B in 2024, USD 4.03B in 2025, projected to reach USD 7.05B by 2030 with 11.8% CAGR. Major drivers include rising cancer incidence, precision medicine adoption, and technological advancements. Liquid biopsy plays key role in early disease detection, oncology care, and personalized medicine. Expanding beyond oncology into prenatal testing and organ transplantation monitoring. Innovations like NGS and digital PCR enhancing market growth.
data.description: The global liquid biopsy market is experiencing significant growth, with a value of US$4.03 billion in 2025 and projected to reach US$7.05 billion by 2030, at a CAGR of 11.8%. Factors driving this surge include increasing cancer incidence, adoption of precision medicine, and technological advancements. Liquid biopsy offers a minimally invasive alternative to tissue biopsies, allowing for the detection of circulating tumor DNA and other biomarkers through a blood test. This provides real-time insights for timely treatment decisions, especially important as cancer cases climb globally. The technology is expanding beyond oncology into areas like prenatal testing and organ transplantation monitoring. Innovations like next-generation sequencing and digital PCR are enhancing the sensitivity and clinical utility of liquid biopsy assays, making it an essential tool in healthcare.

data.title: Inspira Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd. gains U.S. Patent for ART500 core tech, securing lead in $20B market for respiratory support.
data.description: Inspira™ Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd., based in Ra'anana, Israel, a leading company in life-support and diagnostic technologies, has recently obtained a U.S. Patent for a low flow rates extracorporeal oxygenation system and its methods of application. This patent is crucial as it covers the foundational technology of the ART500 tool, which is vital in Inspira's plan to enter the advanced respiratory support market estimated at $20 billion. The patent includes 16 unique claims, solidifying the innovative nature of the technology.

data.title: Workday's stock drops as revenue guidance disappoints despite exceeding earnings expectations in second quarter.
data.description: The stock of Workday experienced a decline following the company's announcement of second-quarter earnings that exceeded expectations. However, the guidance provided by the software maker was in line with analyst forecasts. This news led to a drop in Workday's stock value.

data.title: Nvidia halts H20 production post Beijing directive to stop AI chip purchase in China. NVDA stocks indicated slightly lower.
data.description: According to reports, Nvidia has ceased production of the H20 chip after receiving a directive from Beijing advising local companies against purchasing the AI chip. This decision comes just weeks after the Chinese government's recommendation. As a result of this news, NVDA stock was slightly down.

data.title: Meta Platforms signed a $10 billion, six-year deal with Google parent Alphabet for AI cloud services.
data.description: According to sources cited in a report by The Information, Meta Platforms has recently inked a significant six-year agreement for cloud services with Alphabet, the parent company of Google. The deal, reported late Thursday, is valued at a substantial $10 billion. This partnership signifies a major development in the tech industry, with Meta leveraging Google's AI cloud services. The news was covered by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Zoom's Q2 earnings and revenue surpass estimates, with a rise in enterprise sales; stock up.
data.description: Zoom's stock saw an increase following the report of their Q2 earnings and revenue surpassing estimates. The company's enterprise sales growth served as a notable highlight. The post on Investor's Business Daily highlighted how Zoom's earnings and revenue outperformed expectations, pointing towards the strength of their enterprise segment.

data.title: EnWave releases third quarter financial results for 2025; highlights from quarter ending June 30, 2025.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on August 22, 2025, EnWave Corporation (TSX-V:ENW | FSE:E4U) shared their consolidated interim financial results for the third quarter concluding on June 30, 2025.

data.title: Stock market futures rise ahead of Powell's Jackson Hole speech. 10-year Treasury bond yields 4.34%, 2-year at 3.79%. 71.3% likelihood of rate cut. Major indices up (Dow 0.30%, S&P 500 0.21%, Nasdaq 100 0.12%, Russell 2000 0.48%). SPY and QQQ ETFs up in premarket. Notable declines for Walmart and Coty stocks. Initial jobless claims at 235,000; existing home sales up by 2% in July. Services PMI falls to 55.4, manufacturing PMI rises to 53.3.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose following Thursday's declines as investors awaited Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell's speech at the Jackson Hole Economic Policy Symposium. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.34% and the two-year bond was at 3.79%. Markets priced a 71.3% chance of a rate cut in September. Futures for major indices increased: Dow Jones by 0.30%, S&P 500 by 0.21%, Nasdaq 100 by 0.12%, and Russell 2000 by 0.48%. SPY and QQQ ETFs tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 rose in premarket. Most S&P 500 sectors closed negatively on Thursday, while energy and materials stocks performed well. Walmart shares fell 4.5% after Q2 results, and Coty Inc. dipped 22% due to an EPS miss. Jobless claims rose to 235,000, existing home sales increased, services PMI fell to 55.4, and manufacturing PMI rose to 53.3. Full details on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Boeing's offer rejected as 3,200 machinists strike for higher pay. Company proposed 20% wage increase, bonus, and benefits.
data.description: Boeing Co.'s defense operations in Missouri and Illinois are currently on hold due to a strike by 3,200 machinists who are demanding better pay and benefits. This week, union leaders and a Democratic congressman showed their support for the workers on strike. The strike began after the workers rejected Boeing's contract offer, which included a 20% wage increase, a $5,000 ratification bonus, and additional vacation and sick leave. IAM International President Brian Bryant stated that the union is ready to resume negotiations, although no new talks have been planned yet. Boeing's St. Louis executive, Dan Gillian, defended the company's proposal, highlighting that it would result in an average wage growth of 40%. The strike continues as both sides remain at a standstill.

data.title: Dormant Bitcoin whales shifting $642 million BTC into $267 million ETH ahead of Jackson Hole speech.
data.description: There has been a resurgence in activity among previously inactive Bitcoin whales, as they shift their investments into Ethereum. On-chain data from Lookonchain is reporting that one Bitcoin whale, who acquired 100,784 BTC ($642 million) nearly seven years ago, recently sold off some of their BTC to purchase 62,914 ETH ($267 million) and open a long position of 135,265 ETH ($577 million). Another dormant whale followed a similar strategy by moving BTC into Hyperliquid, with the wallet originally receiving 85,947 BTC ($547 million) and still holding 83,585 BTC ($9.42 billion) across six wallets. These moves coincide with a slight decrease in both Bitcoin and Ethereum prices, prompting traders to take note of Ethereum's relative strength compared to Bitcoin, suggesting potential upward momentum. This shift in investment patterns is being closely monitored by analysts for further insights.

data.title: CleanCore Solutions (ZONE) sees record revenue in Q4 and FY 2025. Q4 US revenue tops $1.1M, FY revenue up 29% to $2.1M.
data.description: Revenue from the United States in the fourth quarter surpassed $1.1 million, marking a significant increase. The total revenue for the full year rose by 29% to reach $2.1 million. These figures showcase strong growth and financial performance for the specified time periods.

data.title: Horizon Aircraft reveals Q4 and full year 2025 performance in latest business update.
data.description: A recent report indicates a significant increase in unemployment rates, with the current figure standing at 6.9%. This marks a stark rise of 2.4% from the previous month. In parallel, the number of new jobless claims has surged to 900,000, surpassing expectations and reflecting the continued economic challenges facing the nation due to the ongoing pandemic. Furthermore, the report highlights a concerning trend as industries struggle to recover and reimagine their workforce amidst the uncertain environment, underscoring the need for targeted support and interventions to stabilize the labor market.

data.title: Hofseth Biocare ASA's Q2 2025 financial report: NOK 68.4M total operating revenue, down from NOK 80.4M same period last year.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, HBC reported a total operating revenue of NOK 68.4 million, a decrease from NOK 80.4 million in the corresponding period of 2024. The net operating revenue for the quarter was NOK 67.8 million, down from NOK 72.2 million in the same quarter of the previous year. The total operating revenue in the first half of 2025 amounted to NOK 129.3 million, slightly lower than the NOK 132.8 million generated in the same period in 2024.
Honkarakenne Oyj's half-year report for Jan 1 - Jun 30, 2025, released. Check it out for detailed financial info.

data.title: Bavarian Nordic reports first half 2025 results in Copenhagen, announces financial calendar for 2026.
data.description: Bavarian Nordic A/S (OMX: BAVA) revealed its interim financial results and business advancements for the initial half of 2025 in Copenhagen, Denmark, on August 22. The company also disclosed its financial schedule for 2026.
Prosafe SE's latest report reveals Q2 earnings and first half of 2025 performance data.
Stockwik releases Q2 2025 report covering January 1 - June 30, revealing key financial figures.

data.title: Dow Jones closes nearly flat. Nasdaq hammered, Palantir and Nvidia big losers. Manufacturing services name sold off.
data.description: On Tuesday, the Dow Jones index ended the day with a small gain, while tech giants Nvidia and Palantir experienced declines. Additionally, a manufacturing services company saw a significant drop in its stock price. The overall market performance saw the Dow Jones nearly unchanged, but the Nasdaq index faced substantial losses. This information was shared in a recent market update with Nvidia and Palantir highlighted as major losers of the day by a leading financial publication.
